target.name: Percepto
target.founder: Dor Abuhasira, Ariel Avitan, Sagi Blonder, Raviv Raz

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $128M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Tekever
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series C)

data.narrative:
Percepto is a Series C-stage autonomous systems company in the Drones & Autonomous sector, having raised $128M to date. The peer universe comprises six private companies in the sector, including Zipline, Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Elroy Air; no direct public comparables exist in this narrow space.

A stage-progression methodology was applied, using the $128M total capital raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C to later-stage advancement. This approach yielded an implied valuation of $218M. A peer-median approach was not applicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow Drones & Autonomous sector—only one adjacent-stage peer was identified, falling short of the three-company threshold needed for reliable median derivation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $185M to $250M, with a central estimate of $218M. This range reflects ±15% sensitivity around the stage-progression result, accounting for methodological constraints. Key uncertainties include the limited peer set and the reliance on a single triangulation method. The absence of public benchmarks and the sparse private comparable universe limit confidence in this valuation and warrant recalibration as the company approaches later funding rounds or exit events that may provide stronger market signals.
